perturbation.original_text: 6. LICENSE FEES. (a) Rogers shall pay to Licensor a fee (the "License Fee"), which shall be equal to fifty (50)% of Retail Revenues. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Retail Revenues" means the retail revenues actually received by Rogers for each authorized exhibition of a Licensed Program on the ROD Service, less GST and applicable sales tax. For greater certainty, Retail Revenues shall not include any fees payable to Rogers for: (i) the purchase, rental or installation of a digital terminal; (ii) any basic or premium television services; or (iii) digital cable access.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. LICENSE FEES. (a) Rogers shall pay to Licensor a fee (the "License Fee"), which shall be determined at Rogers' discretion based on market conditions. For greater certainty, License Fees shall not include any fees payable to Rogers for: (i) the purchase, rental or installation of a digital terminal; (ii) any basic or premium television services; or (iii) digital cable access.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the defined calculation for the License Fee (50% of Retail Revenues less GST and sales tax), replacing it with 'determined at Rogers' discretion based on market conditions'. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 6(b), which outlines specific deductions Rogers is entitled to make from 'Retail Revenues' and 'License Fees', as these terms are now undefined and the initial License Fee calculation has been made ambiguous. This uncertainty affects enforceability, as there's no objective standard to calculate the License Fee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Notwithstanding Section 6(a) above, (i) Prior to calculating License Fees, Rogers shall be entitled to deduct from Retail Revenues on a rolling monthly basis during the Term ("Rogers Costs"): (A) the value of programming credits, rebates or other retail price discounts provided to ROD Service subscribers in conjunction with a public marketing or promotional initiative; -2- Source: EUROMEDIA HOLDINGS CORP, 10SB12G, 2/15/2007 (B) the value of refunds provided by Rogers to ROD Service subscribers for technical or other errors during such month; (C) the percentage equal to Rogers' average year-to-end bad debt exposure (including credit collection costs) for the then current calendar year in respect of all Rogers' cable television subscribers (expressed as a percentage) (D) encoding costs incurred by Rogers, if applicable, pursuant to Section 8 (a) ; and (E) closed captioning costs incurred by Rogers to comply with Applicable Law. (ii) Rogers shall be entitled to deduct from License Fees on a rolling monthly basis during the Term: (A) the marketing allowance pursuant to Section 12; (B) the Production Fund Commitment pursuant to Section 13; and (C) copyright royalty payments pursuant to Section 17.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a concrete calculation for the License Fee (50% of Retail Revenues) with a discretionary determination by Rogers based on 'market conditions'. This directly contradicts Section 6(b), which outlines specific deductions from 'Retail Revenues' and 'License Fees'. Since 'License Fees' are now determined at Rogers' discretion and not directly tied to Retail Revenues in a defined way, the deductions outlined in 6(b) become ambiguous and unenforceable. The original text provided a formulaic basis which is now gone, thus it is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. ENCODING AND DELIVERY OF LICENSED PROGRAMS. (a) Licensor shall, at its sole expense, encode each Licensed Program selected for distribution and exhibition by Rogers hereunder and shall deliver such fully encoded Licensed Program to Rogers on a DLT master tape, DVD-R disc or other master pre-recorded format acceptable to Rogers (each, a "Master") at least thirty (30) days prior to the availability date for such Licensed Program. Such Masters shall at all times remain the sole property of Licensor and, within fifteen (15) days following the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Rogers shall either return to Licensor all such Masters then in its possession, or issue a certificate of erasure.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. ENCODING AND DELIVERY OF LICENSED PROGRAMS. (a) Licensor shall encode each Licensed Program selected for distribution and exhibition by Rogers hereunder and shall deliver such encoded Licensed Program to Rogers on a format to be decided later. Such Masters shall at all times remain the sole property of Licensor and, within fifteen (15) days following the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Rogers shall either return to Licensor all such Masters then in its possession, or issue a certificate of erasure.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'at its sole expense' and changing 'DLT master tape, DVD-R disc or other master pre-recorded format acceptable to Rogers' to 'a format to be decided later', the responsibility for encoding and the specific delivery format become unclear. While the text retains the statement about the 'Masters' remaining the property of the licensor and what to do with them at termination, the removal of key details creates a contradiction, because later on Rogers can require a Master format of delivery that is not possible to provide by the Licensor due to the type of encoding and it having the 'sole property' in Section 8(a), in essence, it can't change the encoding, since it owns it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. ENCODING AND DELIVERY OF LICENSED PROGRAMS. (a) Licensor shall encode each Licensed Program selected for distribution and exhibition by Rogers hereunder and shall deliver such encoded Licensed Program to Rogers on a format to be decided later. Such Masters shall at all times remain the sole property of Licensor and, within fifteen (15) days following the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Rogers shall either return to Licensor all such Masters then in its possession, or issue a certificate of erasure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'at its sole expense' and alters the delivery format specification to 'a format to be decided later.' This creates a contradiction because, while the Licensor still owns the Masters, Rogers can now dictate a delivery format that the Licensor might be unable or unwilling to provide at their own expense, conflicting with the Licensor's ownership rights and potentially placing an unforeseen financial burden on them without prior agreement, impacting the licensor's rights to manage the master copies as stated in the unchanged text.

perturbation.original_text: 13. PRODUCTION FUND. For so long as Rogers is required by Applicable Law to contribute a percentage of its gross annual revenues from the ROD Service to an independently-administered Canadian program production fund, Licensor shall reimburse Rogers for 50% of the amount required to be remitted to such production fund by Rogers in respect of the exhibition of Licensed Programs (the "Production Fund Commitment"). The Production Fund Commitment shall be calculated monthly during the Term and shall be deducted as set out in Section 6 (b).
perturbation.changed_text: 13. PRODUCTION FUND. For so long as Rogers contributes to a Canadian program production fund, Licensor shall reimburse Rogers for a portion of the amount required to be remitted to such production fund by Rogers in respect of the exhibition of Licensed Programs (the "Production Fund Commitment"). The Production Fund Commitment shall be calculated monthly during the Term and shall be deducted as set out in Section 6 (b).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'required by Applicable Law to contribute a percentage of its gross annual revenues from the ROD Service to an independently-administered Canadian program production fund' and '50%', replacing it with 'a portion', the trigger and amount of the Production Fund Commitment become vague. This creates a contradiction because Section 6(b)(ii)(B) still mandates the deduction of 'the Production Fund Commitment' without specifying its calculation, thus making the specific deduction impossible to determine, since the trigger for the fund is up to Rogers to contribute and the amount on Section 13. is also left unspecified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Notwithstanding Section 6(a) above, (i) Prior to calculating License Fees, Rogers shall be entitled to deduct from Retail Revenues on a rolling monthly basis during the Term ("Rogers Costs"): (A) the value of programming credits, rebates or other retail price discounts provided to ROD Service subscribers in conjunction with a public marketing or promotional initiative; -2- Source: EUROMEDIA HOLDINGS CORP, 10SB12G, 2/15/2007 (B) the value of refunds provided by Rogers to ROD Service subscribers for technical or other errors during such month; (C) the percentage equal to Rogers' average year-to-end bad debt exposure (including credit collection costs) for the then current calendar year in respect of all Rogers' cable television subscribers (expressed as a percentage) (D) encoding costs incurred by Rogers, if applicable, pursuant to Section 8 (a) ; and (E) closed captioning costs incurred by Rogers to comply with Applicable Law. (ii) Rogers shall be entitled to deduct from License Fees on a rolling monthly basis during the Term: (A) the marketing allowance pursuant to Section 12; (B) the Production Fund Commitment pursuant to Section 13; and (C) copyright royalty payments pursuant to Section 17.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific trigger ('required by Applicable Law to contribute a percentage of its gross annual revenues from the ROD Service to an independently-administered Canadian program production fund') and fixed amount ('50%') for the Production Fund Commitment. This creates a contradiction with Section 6(b)(ii)(B), which mandates the deduction of the 'Production Fund Commitment' but relies on the now-vague Section 13 to define its calculation, creating an inconsistency. The change now allows Rogers discretionary contribution and the unspecified 'portion' makes it impossible to deduct the right amount.
